_comment: I5 docs-conformance exceptions baseline (Invariant I5). Each entry is a backticked span (from an architecture doc) that datrix/scripts/test/check-docs-conformance.py cannot resolve to a real file/directory/module, verified to be legitimately non-existent (a migration-history 'what was removed' claim, a negative/prohibition claim, or another confirmed-intentional non-existence) rather than a genuine drift defect. A NEW entry here is a deliberate, reviewed exception -- never a way to silence a script complaint without reading why it fired.

exceptions.reason: migration-history reference to a deliberately-removed file; the doc's own text states it was removed
migration-history reference to a deliberately-removed file; the doc's own text states it was removed
migration-history reference to a deliberately-removed file; the doc's own text states it was removed
migration-history reference to a deliberately-removed file; the doc's own text states it was removed
negative/prohibition claim ('must never contain a tests/ pytest suite'), not an existence assertion

migration-history reference: the doc's own text states this hand-authored per-package declaration list 'and its siblings are deleted', replaced by parity/derived_declarations.py's derive_domain_declarations()
